findings.description: ContextProvider protocol was specified in the proposal and capability-resolver spec but not implemented. CapabilitySet.context was typed as Any instead of ContextProvider.
findings.resolution: Added ContextProvider protocol in capabilities/context.py with DefaultContextProvider wrapping compose_system_prompt(). Updated CapabilitySet.context type, resolver, and tests.

findings.description: Harness-adapter spec declared create_agent(capabilities: CapabilitySet) signature, but implementation retained P1 create_agent(tools, extensions) signature. This was a deliberate design choice — migrating to CapabilitySet-based invocation is deferred to P2 when concrete MemoryPolicy implementations exist.
findings.resolution: Updated harness-adapter spec delta to match the implemented signature. Added note that migration to CapabilitySet invocation is deferred to P2 memory-architecture.

findings.description: Tool-policy spec declared authorized_tools(persona, role) without loaded_extensions parameter, and specified that authorized_extensions() calls PersonaRegistry.load_extensions() internally. Implementation correctly passes loaded_extensions as a keyword argument to keep ToolPolicy decoupled from PersonaRegistry.
findings.resolution: Updated tool-policy spec delta to match the implemented signature with loaded_extensions keyword argument. Removed PersonaRegistry coupling requirement.

findings.description: CapabilityResolver.resolve() has duplicated construction logic for sdk vs host branches — both branches construct guardrails/tools/context identically, differing only in sandbox and memory providers.
findings.resolution: Acceptable for clarity — the duplication makes the two branches independently readable. Refactoring into shared construction would obscure the tier-specific differences.

findings.description: DelegationSpawner.harness type annotation changed from HarnessAdapter to SdkHarnessAdapter. This is correct (delegation only applies to SDK harnesses) but is technically a breaking change for any code passing a raw HarnessAdapter.
findings.resolution: Acceptable — no external consumers exist. The __init__.py re-exports provide backward compat for import paths.

findings.description: _HostProvidedSandbox is defined as a private class in resolver.py rather than alongside PassthroughSandbox in sandbox.py. This creates a pattern inconsistency — all other host-provided stubs live in their respective protocol modules.
findings.resolution: Minor — could be moved to sandbox.py in a future cleanup. Not blocking.
